commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Not covered by most private plans. Import or buy at Farmacias Arrocha. Pensionado 15% discount may apply.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Generic available. Pensionado 15% discount. May need specific brand from Panama City.

commonMedications.name: Montelukast [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Available at major pharmacies. Pensionado 15% discount.

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: May need to source from larger pharmacies in David or Panama City.

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Available at most pharmacies. Pensionado 15% discount.

commonMedications.name: Topiramate [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Generic available. May need to source from David or Panama City.

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Excellent in Panama City. Major chains include Farmacias Arrocha, Super Farmacia, and Farmacias Metro. Many medications available without prescription. 24-hour pharmacies available in major areas.
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Prescriptions from licensed Panamanian doctors. Many medications available OTC that require prescription in the US. CSS (social security) system provides medications at low cost if enrolled; private patients pay out-of-pocket or through insurance. Pensionado visa holders receive 15% discount on prescription medications.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Private insurance is standard for expats. Plans from $200-400/mo for seniors. Major providers: ASSA, Mapfre, BlueCross Panama. Most plans cover 70-80% of medications after deductible. Pre-existing conditions may be excluded for first 1-2 years.

annualExamCost.notes: Private ophthalmologists $40-60 per visit. Pensionado discount 15% on medical consultations.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Most private plans cover annual eye exams. Glasses and contacts typically not covered, or limited to $100-150 annual allowance. Pensionado 15% discount applies to eye exams and some optical shops.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-2 weeks for private ophthalmologists. Same-day appointments sometimes available. Clinica Boyd and Hospital Nacional are top eye care facilities.
vision.seniorConsiderations: Excellent private ophthalmology. Cataract surgery $1,500-2,500 per eye (vs $3,500+ in US). LASIK available. Pensionado discount reduces consultation costs. Many US-trained specialists.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Pensionado discount 15% on dental procedures. Many US-trained dentists.

commonProcedures.coverageNote: Pensionado 15% discount. Private dental plans cover basic fillings.
Pensionado discount applies. Porcelain crowns at higher end.
Pensionado 15% discount. About 50-60% cheaper than US prices.
Simple extraction at lower end; surgical at higher end. Pensionado discount applies.
Significant savings vs US ($3,000-5,000). Many expats come to Panama specifically for implants.
Full set at typical price. Pensionado 15% discount reduces cost further.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Separate dental insurance available $30-60/mo. Some health plans include basic dental. Pensionado visa provides 15% discount on all dental services regardless of insurance. Out-of-pocket costs still much lower than US.
dental.dentistAvailability: Excellent in Panama City. Many US-trained dentists with modern equipment. English widely spoken at expat-oriented practices. Dental tourism is growing. Clinics in Punta Pacifica and El Cangrejo areas popular with expats.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Jubilado (retiree) discounts 10-25% at many restaurants by law.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant: ~$22 + 7% ITBMS + 10% tip = ~$27, 2x/week = ~$235/mo

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: Jubilado discount 25% on utilities including internet.

categories.name: NFL RedZone
categories.notes: DAZN NFL Game Pass ~$11/mo during season (Sep-Feb). Annualized ~$10/mo. Includes RedZone, live games, NFL Network.

monthlyPass.notes: No monthly pass per se. Metro and MetroBus use rechargeable RapiPass card. $0.35/bus ride, $0.35/metro ride. Estimate based on 2 rides/day.

publicTransit.coverage: Metro Line 1 (north-south) and Line 2 (east to Nuevo Tocumen). MetroBus covers most of the city. Mi Bus feeder routes. Coverage expanding with Line 3 under construction.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Pensionado/jubilado RapiPass card offers free metro and bus rides. Must register at Metro office with Pensionado visa documentation.

rideShare.availability: Uber widely used. DiDi also available. Regular yellow taxis use zones (not meters). InDriver app popular for negotiated fares.
rideShare.notes: Taxi from Tocumen airport to city center $30-35 fixed rate. Uber typically $20-25. Traffic congestion can double trip costs during peak hours.

registration.notes: Annual vehicle inspection (revisado) required. Registration through ATTT (Autoridad de Tránsito).

insurance.notes: Liability insurance mandatory. ASSA, Mapfre, ASSA are major providers. Higher rates for imported vehicles.

fuel.notes: Government-regulated fuel prices. Gasoline approximately $0.90-1.00/liter. Traffic congestion increases fuel consumption significantly.

parking.notes: Building parking included in many apartment rentals. Mall parking usually free. Downtown street parking metered.

tolls.notes: Corredor Sur and Corredor Norte toll roads $0.50-1.00 per trip. Frequent use adds up. Many commuters use them to avoid traffic.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: No specific senior car ownership discounts. Pensionado 15% discount on auto insurance premiums at some providers.

transportation.walkability: Varies greatly by neighborhood. Casco Viejo very walkable. Cinta Costera waterfront path excellent. Many neighborhoods have poor sidewalks and are car-dependent. Heat and rain can make walking uncomfortable.

cycling.infrastructure: Cinta Costera has a dedicated bike path. Otherwise cycling infrastructure is minimal and traffic is aggressive. Not recommended as primary transportation.
cycling.notes: Cinta Costera bike path is pleasant for exercise. Commuter cycling is dangerous due to driver behavior and road conditions.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Better EV selection than Boquete. BYD, Hyundai, Kia, Nissan dealers. Some Tesla grey imports. Import duty exemption under Law 295.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Law 295 (2022): EVs exempt from import duties and ITBMS (7% sales tax). Reduced annual vehicle tax. Growing government support.

chargingHome.notes: Electricity ~$0.15-0.20/kWh. Most condos allow home charging installation.

chargingPublic.notes: Growing network in Panama City. Chargers at Multiplaza, Albrook Mall, some hotels. EV adoption accelerating.

insurance.notes: Better options than Boquete. ASSA, Mapfre, SURA. Pensionado discount may apply.

maintenance.notes: Better EV service access than Boquete. Authorized dealer service available in the city.

registration.notes: Reduced vehicle tax for EVs under Law 295.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: Manufacturer standard. Better warranty service access in Panama City.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: Larger EV market in the city. Better resale prospects than Boquete.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($35), insurance ($85), maintenance ($38/mo avg), registration ($2/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.description: Discount on meals at participating restaurants
discounts.details: Applies to Pensionado/jubilado visa holders. Must present Pensionado card. Most mid-range and chain restaurants participate.
Applies to all Pensionado card holders. Cinemas, theaters, and events must honor the discount.

discounts.description: Doctor visits, lab work, hospital fees
discounts.details: Applies at private clinics and hospitals. Must present Pensionado card. Some doctors apply automatically.
Applies at private dental clinics. Combined with already lower Panama prices, significant savings vs US.
Applies at all licensed pharmacies. Farmacias Arrocha and Super Farmacia honor the discount.
Applies to domestic Copa flights and some regional carriers. Must book in person or call to apply discount.
Register at Metro de Panamá office with Pensionado documentation to receive the special card.

discounts.discount: 25% off electricity (up to first 600 kWh); 25% off phone
discounts.details: Applied to residential electricity bills automatically once registered. Phone discount on landline service.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Panama at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: +Movil Postpaid (Local)
plans.notes: +Movil postpaid $9/mo per line + 7% ITBMS. 3GB data, unlimited calls. Excellent 5G coverage in Panama City.

housing.taxNotes: No rental tax in Panama. Property tax (impuesto de inmueble) is landlord responsibility with generous exemptions for new construction (up to 20 years tax-free). No VAT on residential rent. Panama uses USD (balboa pegged 1:1).
housing.leaseTerms: Typical 1-year lease. Security deposit usually 1 month + last month rent. Panama Civil Code governs rentals. Pensionado visa holders get 25% off utility bills.
housing.marketNotes: Panama City has both high-rise apartments and standalone houses. Houses more common in suburbs: Clayton, Ciudad del Saber, Albrook, Costa del Este. Gated communities (residenciales) popular with expats. AC essential year-round.

stores.name: Mercado de Mariscos
stores.notes: Famous seafood market on Cinta Costera

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: No public healthcare for expats. Private insurance required. Pre-existing condition exclusions common for first 12-24 months. Pensionado visa: 15-25% discount on medical services.

costs.notes: $225-275/mo. Pre-existing exclusions for AMD, obesity. Age loading for 60+. BUPA, Cigna, or local Pacific plans.
$800-1200/injection, 6-8/yr. Likely excluded from insurance. Must travel to Panama City or David.
$60-100/visit in Panama City. Limited specialists in Boquete.
15% off medical consultations, 10-15% off hospital services.
$180-220/mo. Pre-existing exclusion period for diabetes (12-24 months).
Glucose monitor, strips, lancets. Limited insurance coverage initially.
May need to travel to Panama City for specialists.

shared.notes: Good dental care in David at $40-80/visit. Pensionado 15% discount.
Specialist visits may require travel from Boquete to David (45 min) or Panama City.
Higher out-of-pocket risk with pre-existing exclusions.

healthcare.notes: First 1-2 years will have higher costs due to pre-existing condition exclusions on insurance. Anti-VEGF injections are the largest single expense — limited providers outside Panama City.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Panama City has a large Afro-Panamanian community, especially in neighborhoods like Colón, Curundú, and San Miguelito. Good salon options.

providers.name: African Queen Beauty Salon
providers.notes: Popular Afro salon. Braids, weaves, locs, natural hair, color. Experienced stylists.

providers.name: Salon Rizos
providers.notes: Specializes in curly and coily hair. DevaCurl, color services. Modern salon.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Panama City has upscale salons in Obarrio and Punta Pacifica areas, popular with expats.

providers.name: Salon Enrique Iglesias
providers.notes: Top Panama City salon. Expert colorists. Highlights, balayage. Expat-friendly.

providers.name: David's Salon
providers.notes: Good color work, English-speaking staff. Popular with expat community.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: Albrook Mall and Multiplaza have the best selection. Extended sizes more available than in Boquete.

providers.name: Albrook Mall
providers.notes: Largest mall in Latin America. Multiple stores with extended sizes. Very affordable.

providers.name: Multiplaza Pacific
providers.notes: Upscale shopping. Zara, H&M with larger sizes. Better quality options.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Panama City has excellent shopping. Albrook Mall for budget, Multiplaza for mid-range to upscale.

providers.name: Albrook Mall
providers.notes: Hundreds of stores. Budget to mid-range. Full regular sizes.

providers.name: Multiplaza Pacific
providers.notes: Zara, Mango, international brands. Good selection of regular women's sizes.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Good shoe shopping in Panama City. Cheaper than US prices. Wide widths still limited.

providers.name: Albrook Mall shoe stores
providers.notes: Many shoe stores. Budget to mid-range. Sizes vary by store.

providers.name: Payless / Hush Puppies
providers.notes: Standard shoe retailers. Moderate prices. Extended sizes in some styles.
